findings.summary: v1.0 'op:cc-pipeline-keystone' resolved by Theorem 3.3.D (V_quad CC-channel recovery) and split into op:cc-formal-borel (residual Laplace step) plus op:cc-monodromy-RH (Stokes-matrix closure for the five non-V_quad families).

Title promoted from 'Channel structure of asymptotic deformations in PCF dynamics: an outline' to 'Channel theory for polynomial continued fractions: asymptotic channels, the xi_0 = 2/sqrt(beta_2) identity, and a bridge conjecture'. Reflects that v1.1 is no longer just an outline; it carries Proposition 3.3.A (proved) and Theorem 3.3.D (modulo Borel-Laplace).

Bridge conjecture re-parametrised by coefficient tuples (alpha_1, alpha_0, beta_2, beta_1, beta_0) rather than by discriminant alone, with three observed tiers B1/B2/B3. v1.0's Delta-driven framing collapsed data the channel does not collapse (e.g. V_quad and QL15 share xi_0 but differ in Delta).
